explanation_md: `N` is a compile-time constant. `Repeated<3>` sets `value = 3 * 3 = 9`; `Repeated<5>` sets `value = 5 * 5 = 25`. The initializer runs at object construction but uses the template constant.

prompt_md: Which of the following is a valid non-type template argument?

options.b: An `int` variable declared at local scope in `main()`.

explanation_md: Non-type template arguments must be compile-time constants. Integer literals satisfy this requirement. Local variables, `std::string` objects, and runtime floating-point results do not — they are not known until the program runs.

options.a: `buf_[head_]` accesses a private member, which is not allowed outside the class.
options.b: The out-of-class definitions use `template <typename T>` and `Ring<T>::` instead of `template <typename T, int N>` and `Ring<T, N>::`, omitting the non-type parameter.
options.c: `N` cannot be used in a `return` statement of a member function.
options.d: The class body uses a brace-initializer `{}` on `buf_`, which is not allowed for raw arrays.

explanation_md: The out-of-class definitions drop `int N` from the template parameter list and `N` from the qualifier. Both `template <typename T, int N>` and `Ring<T, N>::` are required. The compiler reports a mismatch between the declaration and the out-of-class definition.

options.a: Yes — they share the same type parameter `int`, so they are the same template instantiation.
options.b: No — each unique combination of template arguments produces a distinct type.
options.c: It depends on whether `FixedArray` defines a conversion operator.
options.d: Yes — the non-type parameter is erased at compile time, leaving identical types.

explanation_md: Every unique set of template arguments produces a completely separate type. `FixedArray<int, 5>` and `FixedArray<int, 10>` are as different as `int` and `double`. Non-type parameters are part of the type identity — they are never erased.
Three values are pushed: `top_` increments to 3. `top()` returns `data_[top_ - 1]` = `data_[2]` = 30. The output is `3 30`.
